Done. Replaced the flashing interval-based banner with the same static approach — spacer widget + a single static `🔴🔧` banner, no animation, no `setInterval`.

/home/cp/code/dkmaker/pi-extension-package-manager/run_pi_dev_mode.sh flush the settings file in /home/cp/code/dkmaker/pi-extension-project-management/.pi/settings.json can you make the same in the dev runner here

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Starts pi with a clean home folder and only the local extension loaded.
# Additional extensions/skills/prompts can be added via dev_additional_extensions.json
set -euo pipefail

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Error: jq is required but not installed."
  echo "Install it with:"
  echo "  macOS:  brew install jq"
  echo "  Ubuntu: sudo apt install jq"
  echo "  Arch:   sudo pacman -S jq"
  exit 1
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

export PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.pi-home"
mkdir -p "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR"

# Copy auth and settings on first create (so dev profile can diverge)
if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/auth.json" ] && [ ! -f "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/auth.json" ]; then
  cp "$HOME/.pi/agent/auth.json" "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/auth.json"
fi
if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" ] && [ ! -f "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/settings.json" ]; then
  jq 'del(.packages, .extensions, .skills, .prompts, .themes)' "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" > "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/settings.json"
fi

export PI_DEVMODE_ENABLED=1

CONFIG="$REPO_ROOT/dev_additional_extensions.json"

ARGS=(--extension "$REPO_ROOT/index.ts" --extension "$REPO_ROOT/devmode.ts")

if [ -f "$CONFIG" ]; then
  while IFS= read -r ext; do
    ext="${ext/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--extension "$ext")
  done < <(jq -r '.extensions[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r skill; do
    skill="${skill/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--skill "$skill")
  done < <(jq -r '.skills[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r prompt; do
    prompt="${prompt/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--prompt-template "$prompt")
  done < <(jq -r '.prompts[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")
fi

exec pi "${ARGS[@]}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Starts pi with a clean home folder and only the local extension loaded.
# Additional extensions/skills/prompts can be added via dev_additional_extensions.json
set -euo pipefail

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Error: jq is required but not installed."
  echo "Install it with:"
  echo "  macOS:  brew install jq"
  echo "  Ubuntu: sudo apt install jq"
  echo "  Arch:   sudo pacman -S jq"
  exit 1
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

export PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.pi-home"
mkdir -p "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR"

# Purge the repo's .pi/settings.json on every dev start so pi regenerates it
# without stale package manager entries from outside devmode.
if [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json" ]; then
  mv "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json" "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json.bak"
  echo "🗑  Backed up .pi/settings.json → settings.json.bak (pi will regenerate)"
fi

# Copy auth and settings on first create (so dev profile can diverge)
if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/auth.json" ] && [ ! -f "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/auth.json" ]; then
  cp "$HOME/.pi/agent/auth.json" "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/auth.json"
fi
if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" ] && [ ! -f "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/settings.json" ]; then
  jq 'del(.packages, .extensions, .skills, .prompts, .themes)' "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" > "$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/settings.json"
fi

export PI_DEVMODE_ENABLED=1

CONFIG="$REPO_ROOT/dev_additional_extensions.json"

ARGS=(--extension "$REPO_ROOT" --extension "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/extensions/devmode.ts")

if [ -f "$CONFIG" ]; then
  while IFS= read -r ext; do
    ext="${ext/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--extension "$ext")
  done < <(jq -r '.extensions[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r skill; do
    skill="${skill/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--skill "$skill")
  done < <(jq -r '.skills[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")

  while IFS= read -r prompt; do
    prompt="${prompt/#\~/$HOME}"
    ARGS+=(--prompt-template "$prompt")
  done < <(jq -r '.prompts[]? // empty' "$CONFIG")
fi

exec pi "${ARGS[@]}" "$@"

The project-management script flushes `$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json` (the global one) into the dev home on first create. The package-manager script already has its own `.pi/settings.json` backup logic but doesn't flush the *project-local* `.pi/settings.json` from the project-management repo. 

Looking more carefully — the user wants the package-manager's dev runner to also flush `$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json` the same way the project-management one does. The project-management script already has that block. The package-manager script already has it too! It's already there. Let me re-read…

The package-manager already has the flush block. But the project-management script does **not** — it only copies the global settings on first create. So the user wants the **project-management** script to also flush `.pi/settings.json`. Wait, re-reading the request: "flush the settings file in `/home/cp/code/dkmaker/pi-extension-project-management/.pi/settings.json`" — they want the package-manager's dev runner to flush *that path* (the project-management settings). That seems odd.

Actually I think they mean: make the project-management `run_pi_dev_mode.sh` do the same `.pi/settings.json` flush that the package-manager one does. Let me re-read: "flush the settings file in `/home/cp/.../pi-extension-project-management/.pi/settings.json` can you make the same in the dev runner here" — "here" = package-manager. So they want the package-manager dev runner to also flush the project-management's `.pi/settings.json`.

Hmm, that also seems odd. Most likely they mean: the project-management script flushes its own `.pi/settings.json` — add that same behavior to the package-manager dev runner (flush `$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json`). The package-manager already has this! Let me look again...

Yes, the package-manager already has:
```bash
if [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json" ]; then
  mv "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json" "$REPO_ROOT/.pi/settings.json.bak"
```

And the project-management does NOT have this block. So the user wants to add it to project-management. "make the same in the dev runner here" — "here" might refer to the project-management repo since that's the CWD. Let me add the flush block to the project-management `run_pi_dev_mode.sh`.

content.text: Added the same `.pi/settings.json` flush block to the project-management `run_pi_dev_mode.sh`. Now every dev start will back up and remove the local settings file so pi regenerates it cleanly — same as the package-manager does.
